races.name: Racing To School Reaches 25 Years Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Country Park
runners.spotlight: Now 0-12 under rules and beginning to look exposed; however, he brings the best form to this maiden and was going easily when falling three out at Sedgefield last time (2m1f maiden); unraced beyond 2m5f under rules but this winning pointer should stay.
runners.comment: Looking exposed but has the best form; he should be fine over this longer trip

runners.name: Royal Rocket
runners.spotlight: Beaten 16l when sixth of eight runners in a soft-ground bumper here; presumably considered a stayer to go straight over nearly 3m on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Sixth of eight in a bumper here; sent over nearly 3m for this hurdle debut

runners.name: Fairye Forth
runners.spotlight: Bought for Â£33,000 after showing lots of promise in Irish points, the win to the tune of 6l; probably a good prospect for Rebecca Curtis.
runners.comment: Bought for Â£33,000 after showing lots of promise in Irish points

runners.name: Tobyshill
runners.spotlight: 3m point winner who was well beaten in a 2m5f novice hurdle at Ascot for the Twiston-Davies yard; still in the same ownership and his close third at Market Rasen (2m4f, good to soft; first-time tongue-tie) represented improvement.
runners.comment: His close third at Market Rasen (2m4f; first-time tongue-tie) represented improvement

runners.name: Seven Kites
runners.spotlight: Led late on in a 3m Larkhill point a month ago (good ground) but the runner-up has been limited under rules; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Winner of a recent point at Larkhill but the runner-up has been limited under rules

runners.name: Equity Release
runners.spotlight: Insufficient promise in two bumpers and a 1m4f AW maiden for previous yard to believe he'll be in the mix on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Insufficient promise in two bumpers and a 1m4f AW maiden for previous yard

runners.name: Jonny Setaside
runners.spotlight: Both runs on soft ground; the latest was over C&D when beaten 15l into fourth behind a very likeable stablemate of Fairye Forth; should have better days ahead of him.
runners.comment: Beaten 15l here last time behind a very likeable stablemate of Fairye Forth

races.name: LSL Racing Horse Sales - Syndication - Leasing Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hurricane Pat
runners.spotlight: Course bumper winner who launched hurdling career with two Sandown wins (2m, good to soft/soft) in the autumn, the latter at Listed level; drops back in trip/grade after a respectable ninth in Grade 1 Turners at Cheltenham (2m5f, good to soft) last month and ought to be hard to beat, despite his big penalty.
runners.comment: 2-4 over hurdles, including Listed win; not beaten far in Cheltenham Grade 1 last month

runners.name: Cosmic Connection
runners.spotlight: Joined Chris Gordon after slow-ground Irish point win in January and followed up at Warwick (2m, good to soft) on last month's hurdle debut; could have a lot more to offer but probably needs Hurricane Pat to fluff his lines today.
runners.comment: Good winner on last month's rules debut at Warwick; the main danger to Hurricane Pat

runners.name: Both Of Us
runners.spotlight: 3-13 on the Flat for George Boughey, the latest win gained in October (2m, AW; RPR 78), but struggled (albeit on soft ground) when making stable/hurdle debut against own age group last month.
runners.comment: Fair on the Flat (three wins) but struggled on recent stable/hurdle debut
runners.owner: Mrs H Watson & Mrs Carol Lee

runners.name: Bowling Shane
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability during four-race Flat career for Kevin Philippart De Foy last year but struggled in Newbury juvenile hurdle for new stable in February.
runners.comment: Showed some ability on the Flat last year but struggled in a juvenile hurdle in February

runners.name: Play On Court
runners.spotlight: Displayed improvement and belied huge odds when fourth of 14 at Exeter (2m, good to soft; first-time blinkers) last month, his second hurdling start; has each-way claims if able to build on that effort here.
runners.comment: Fourth at huge odds in first-time blinkers last month; holds each-way claims here

runners.name: Good Girl Rachel
runners.spotlight: Placed in a slow-ground Irish point in January but tailed off when switched to hurdling here (2m3f, soft) last month; probably one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Placed in Irish point in January but tailed off when switched to hurdling here last month

runners.name: Below The Belt
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on first two hurdling starts (2m3f/2m; both last month); can't be recommended.
runners.comment: Well beaten on first two hurdling starts (2m3f/2m; both last month)

runners.name: Are You Joking
runners.spotlight: Bettered inauspicious rules debut when sixth at Uttoxeter (2m4f, soft) last month and can probably improve again in due course but has a lot to find with the principals on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Second hurdle run was much better than his first; much more is needed again today, though

runners.name: One For Rian
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on Irish point debut in February 2025 but has not shown much in three maiden/novice hurdles for new stable (2m-2m1f); needs to learn to settle better and will be of much greater interest when the handicapper sees fit to give him a mark.
runners.comment: Second on Irish point debut but hasn't shown much over hurdles (three runs)

runners.name: Modern Style
runners.spotlight: Has a good Flat pedigree but was tailed off on debut for Godolphin last spring and also struggled in two juvenile hurdles for Chris Down in the autumn; now with another new stable.
runners.comment: Struggled on Flat debut and in two subsequent juvenile hurdles; now with another new yard

runners.name: Crystal Box
runners.spotlight: Struggled on stable/hurdle debut at Hereford in February and unseated rider mid-race there eight days ago; remains best watched.
runners.comment: Struggled on hurdle debut and unseated rider mid-race on latest outing

races.name: South West Rail Solutions Ltd Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Touquet
runners.spotlight: Seemingly aided by new tongue-tie when winning twice at Huntingdon (2m, good to soft/soft) during the winter, and was not discredited when second to revitalised subsequent winner at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft) last month; sometimes races a bit too freely but should be fine on this left-handed track; respected.
runners.comment: 2-3 since fitted with a tongue-tie; has conditions to suit; respected

runners.name: Supreme George
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won three in a row in December/January (1m7f-2m, good-good to soft), following a wind op; recent efforts suggest he's still in good form and likely to give this a good shot.
runners.comment: Front-runner; won three in a row during the winter and is still in good form; big player

runners.name: Brulure Noire
runners.spotlight: 3-12 over fences; on a good mark now but returned from a break with a disappointing run in December (first start after wind op) and has not raced since; would probably be better suited by slower ground and/or a longer trip; others preferred.
runners.comment: Absent since poor run in December and probably wants a stiffer test than this

runners.name: Diligent Militia
runners.spotlight: Won small-field Irish point on debut in October 2024 (good ground) and also showed promise in two of his three novice/maiden hurdle runs last autumn; makes chase/handicap debut after a four-month break but sports new tongue-tie/cheekpieces and remains unexposed; market helpful.
runners.comment: Point winner; showed promise over hurdles; makes chase/handicap debut here

runners.name: Le Gentleman
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who was second in a heavy-ground chase in France (2m2f) in September; made low-key British debut in novice hurdle in February but market support would make him of interest.
runners.comment: Second over fences in France last year; made low-key British debut in a novice hurdle
runners.owner: The North West National Hunt Racing Club

races.name: Russell Property Consultants Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Knight Of Allen
runners.spotlight: 1-5 over fences; bumped into a handicap blot when 14l second at Newbury (extended 2m7f, heavy) and early mistakes/interference had him on the back foot when only 11-1 for the Ultima at Cheltenham (4lb out of the weights); with trip/ground fine, he can go well here.
runners.comment: Never gave his running in the Ultima; with trip/ground fine, he can go well here

runners.name: Ben Solo
runners.spotlight: A regular here in this first season over fences and might have won for a second time in this discipline but for unseating three out over Christmas; never really sighted when 33-1 for the Jack Richards at Cheltenham (2m4f, good) in which he was 5lb out of the weights; retains potential and a tongue-tie is added.
runners.comment: Progressive here in this first season over fences; faced a tough task at Cheltenham

runners.name: Jaipaletemps
runners.spotlight: Has won five of his eight chases since November (2m-2m3f); he has predictably shot up the weights and all wins have come under Rian Corcoran who was claiming 7lb or 10lb; therefore, has a lot more on his plate now he's raised in grade off a 5lb higher mark than last time.
runners.comment: Had a season to remember but off current mark and at this level, he'll need another PB

runners.name: Torneo
runners.spotlight: Competitive in all his runs this season but has edged up the weights in the process and all his form is at about 2m; may need to find improvement for this longer trip if he's to defy current mark in this competitive a contest.
runners.comment: May need to find improvement for this longer trip if he's to defy current mark

runners.name: Soldierofthestorm
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner; went one better than on chase debut when making all in a small field at Sandown (2m4f, soft) and a 5lb rise seems fair given the manner in which he won; has won on good ground.
runners.comment: Jumped well in front when winning at Sandown and that only his second chase run; up 5lb

runners.name: Miralago
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles and fences in France; kicked off for Georgina Nicholls with three respectable chase runs towards end of last year (2m-2m4f) and probably best to forgive him his no show over Cheltenham's cross-country course when he was out of the weights; the visor is now.
runners.comment: Five wins in France; solid start in Britain and latest blip was in a cross-country race

runners.name: Blow Your Wad
runners.spotlight: This 8yo arrives in career-best form after his staying-on efforts in Newbury's Greatwood Gold Cup (2m4f) and the Ultima at Cheltenham (3m1f; again on good to soft; won on good); has to be feared, for all that he's 4lb higher than for those runs and 2m3f is on the tight side for him.
runners.comment: Career-best efforts in defeat in strong handicaps the last twice, at 2m4f and 3m1f

runners.name: Olivers Travels
runners.spotlight: Fell early in a strong Ascot handicap when last seen in November; had been running really well on his favoured good ground, failing only narrowly to bring up a hat-trick bid at Kelso (3m2f); however, that's more his trip and 2m3f looks plenty sharp enough.
runners.comment: Last year went well but he's a strong stayer and 2m3f looks plenty sharp enough

races.name: South West Rail Solutions Ltd Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Marlacoo
runners.spotlight: Won an Irish bumper and added an Ayr maiden hurdle last season; beaten seven times since that last win despite heading the market but did run well with the visor back on at Haydock (2m3f, good to soft) four weeks ago; should be involved once more.
runners.comment: Proving expensive to follow but did run well again at Haydock four weeks ago (third)

runners.name: Bluegrass
runners.spotlight: Record of 5-23 over hurdles and placed a few times as well; a bit up and down this season but ran respectably in the new blinkers at Fakenham last time and now regular cheekpieces return over a longer trip that may suit him better.
runners.comment: Fair last run; now regular cheekpieces return over a longer trip that may suit him better

runners.name: Beau Quali
runners.spotlight: His only win came over fences when trained by Dan Skelton; both runs for this yard have been over hurdles, tailed off in the first but then a strong-finishing second at Hereford (2m3f, good); if as good again then he won't be far away.
runners.comment: Second run for this yard when a strong-finishing runner-up at Hereford

runners.name: So You Know
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden hurdle winner (on good ground); runner-up on last month's handicap debut at Warwick (2m, good to soft) when, under a 10lb claimer, he was unable to reel in the front-running winner; Tom Bellamy takes over and he's up in trip; player.
runners.comment: Second on handicap debut; Tom Bellamy takes over and he's up in trip; player

runners.name: Gold For Alec
runners.spotlight: Ran well in defeat on his reappearance but nowhere near as effective in his last four races; the cheekpieces tried last time are removed.
runners.comment: Ran well in defeat on his reappearance but nowhere near as effective in last four races

runners.name: Hackney Hill
runners.spotlight: Just a smidgen of promise in Irish maiden hurdles; he was returning from 425 days off when a 22-1 chance for stable/handicap debut at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft) and nor was that run particularly encouraging; clearly needs to be seen in a very different light to have any sort of chance.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 8yo in a top yard but his form claims are weak

runners.name: Wolf Moon
runners.spotlight: Dual 2m novice hurdle winner last season but well beaten on handicap debut in April and he's also struggled in two chase runs this winter; still lightly raced and now returns after wind surgery, but likely best watched for now.
runners.comment: Chasing this season; not exposed over hurdles and he's had a wind op

runners.name: Chief Sunday
runners.spotlight: Potentially well handicapped but he tried chasing earlier this season and hasn't offered a lot back over hurdles; it looks significant that regular rider Sam Twiston-Davies has turned elsewhere.
runners.comment: Out of his form and his regular rider Sam Twiston-Davies has looked elsewhere

runners.name: American Land
runners.spotlight: Has won five of his last seven races and he might be high in the weights now having come up well short (beaten 13l) on final sighting at Newton Abbot in October (2m1f); on the plus side, he's back at Class 4 level and has won over this far; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Had a fine season but fourth last time and might be high in the weights now

runners.name: Authentic Legacy
runners.spotlight: Only 3lb higher than for sole career win in Newbury handicap hurdle 13 months ago (2m, soft; fine on good ground), which was only three starts back; didn't run too badly on his chase debut on reappearance after a break of 337 days; ran just okay when cheekpieces were tried once before but hard to rule out.
runners.comment: Chase debut on reappearance; only 3lb higher than for last season's win over hurdles

runners.name: Lord Of All Saints
runners.spotlight: He was completing a hat-trick last season when beating Marlacoo at Sandown (2m4f, soft; effective on good to soft); however, there's been no sign of that form this campaign and his usual hood is removed.
runners.comment: Hat-trick last season but there's been no sign of that form this campaign

runners.name: Its Top
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who chased home Sober Glory on his hurdle debut and then made all for a novice win at odds-on at Carlisle in February (2m1f, good to soft); trainer could offer no explanation for his poor handicap debut at Uttoxeter but he's better than that.
runners.comment: Bumper winner and added a novice two runs back; handicap debut was too bad to be true

runners.name: Torrent
runners.spotlight: 2-15 over hurdles; winless this campaign but it started quite well and the third at Sandown in December was in a first-time visor that now returns having been missing the last twice; on a fair mark if factoring in Murray Dodd's claim; could stage a revival.
runners.comment: Off this mark and with the visor back on, he could easily register a return to form

races.name: UB40 Live After Racing 24th July Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: The Italian Fox
runners.spotlight: Just a respectable fifth when upped to 3m for handicap debut in December but displayed big jolt of improvement over C&D (good to soft) a week ago, drawing clear to win comfortably by 14l; 7lb ahead of the handicapper under 7lb penalty today; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Big jolt of improvement when winning by 14l over C&D last week; 7lb well in under penalty

runners.name: Bobalot
runners.spotlight: Well beaten before last-fence refusal on latest outing in February but has a largely consistent record over hurdles; will be suited by the trip/ground and has slipped to a good mark; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Refused in a chase last time but has largely consistent record over hurdles; e-w claims

runners.name: Dessie Haze
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles; not disgraced when sixth of 17 at Exeter (2m7f, good to soft) last month and remains unexposed over this sort of trip but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles; not disgraced at Exeter last month but others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Groom De Cotte
runners.spotlight: Won over fences at Huntingdon (2m7f, good to soft) last March and returned from break with pleasing fourth over hurdles there (3m1f, good to soft) in November; absent for another 131 days since but won't be far away if in similar form again.
runners.comment: Back from break with good effort in November; off another 131 days since; still considered

runners.name: Man Maid
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only five hurdle runs and wasn't beaten far when fourth of six at Ffos Las (2m4f, soft) last month; needs improvement but that's possible if the new trip suits.
runners.comment: Not beaten far over 2m4f last month and remains unexposed; tries a new trip today

runners.name: Galassian
runners.spotlight: Won a soft-ground maiden (2m3f) last season but a subsequent spell over fences was disappointing, and he has also looked out of sorts back over hurdles this term (three runs; cheekpieces added last time); eased in grade today; needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Out of sorts this season (three runs) and needs to turn a corner

runners.name: Kingston Kid
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on old Irish form but lightly raced and disappointing in recent years; swaps cheekpieces for refitted blinkers.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and disappointing in recent years

runners.name: Home And Dry
runners.spotlight: Won low-grade 2m AW handicap on latest outing in October but hasn't run over hurdles since 33l defeat on handicap debut at Hereford (2m5f, good to soft) in February last year, and his profile is somewhat unconvincing.
runners.comment: First hurdle run since 33l defeat on h'cap debut in February 2025; Flat winner in October

runners.name: Ffree Pedro
runners.spotlight: Won three points in spring 2024 but, following a long absence, has been pulled up on all three starts under rules for new stable this season; has too much to prove.
runners.comment: Pulled up all three starts since returned to racing under rules; too much to prove

races.name: Inittogether Festival 22nd-24th May Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Order On Time
runners.spotlight: Out of Flat-winning sibling to scorers under both codes; clear second behind the market leader in soft-ground Irish point and subsequently sold for Â£200,000 to join Paul Nicholls, who has a particularly good strike-rate in Chepstow bumpers; brings potential to rules racing; respected.
runners.comment: Clear second in Irish point; subsequently sold for Â£200,000; brings potential; respected

runners.name: Louxor De Grugy
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred 5yo who showed promise with his fourth of 14 at Kempton (2m, good to soft) where he disputed the lead until running out of steam; one of the main form contenders.
runners.comment: Nicely bred; showed promise with fourth of 14 at Kempton; one of the main form players

runners.name: Moscow Star
runners.spotlight: Identical efforts on AW at Lingfield and soft ground at Doncaster, finishing fifth and registering an RPR of 88 both times; likely set for a similar result unless he finds progress.
runners.comment: Couple of fifth-place finishes; set for similar result unless he improves

runners.name: Miss Roc On Sal
runners.spotlight: Always towards the rear and finished only eighth of nine at Ascot (1m7f, good to soft) but was beaten only about 5l; well bred and may do better with the experience under her belt.
runners.comment: Only eighth of nine at Ascot but is well bred and may do better
runners.owner: C Bennett W Bennett And J Buckley

runners.name: Winmeafortune
runners.spotlight: Newcomer who may be more interesting further down the line, being a half-sister to 2m4f hurdle winners Lady Harriett and Lady Henrietta and out of a 2m3f-2m7f hurdle/chase scorer.
runners.comment: Newcomer whose pedigree suggests she's one for further down the line

runners.name: Gaelic Heather
runners.spotlight: Well-related dam placed in bumper; absent and sold for Â£50,000 since showing ability with second-place finish in Irish point a year ago; check the market signals for further guidance.
runners.comment: Absent and sold for Â£50,000 since showing ability in Irish point a year ago

runners.name: Chance A Glance
runners.spotlight: Dam bumper/2m hurdle winner for his stable, half-sister to high-class 2m2f-2m5f hurdle/chase scorer Master Tommytucker; wears tongue-tie on debut; attractively bred and looks shortlist material.
runners.comment: Out of a bumper/hurdle winner for his stable; looks the pick of the newcomers

runners.name: El Dorado
runners.spotlight: Â£6,000 3yo; dam 7.4f/1m6f Flat (RPR 72) and 2m-2m4f hurdle scorer, out of unraced sibling to winners under both codes; attempts to do the unprecedented, with yard 0-21 in bumpers.
runners.comment: Â£6,000 3yo; related to winners under both codes; yard 0-21 in bumpers

runners.name: Bath Taverness
runners.spotlight: Bred to blossom over hurdles and longer trips; ran accordingly (made only late headway from a detached position) in recording a duck egg at Hereford in the autumn.
runners.comment: Recorded a duck egg at Hereford in the autumn

runners.name: Pink Perjury
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on heavy going at Wincanton; needs major improvement for the switch to forecast faster ground.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on heavy ground at Wincanton

runners.name: Graciousandplenty
runners.spotlight: Dropped away and beat only one rival at Newbury where she was the rank outsider; others preferred.
runners.comment: Dropped away and beat only one rival at Newbury

runners.name: Topsham Tide
runners.spotlight: Beaten long way on soft ground at Exeter in sole start for Chris Down; remains in same ownership; best watched unless the betting foretells a bigger effort.
runners.comment: Beaten long way on soft ground in sole start for Chris Down

races.name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Smoke Trail
runners.spotlight: 0-3 since winning on hurdle debut here (2m, good to soft) in October but he ran okay when fourth of six in a handicap at Newbury (2m good to soft) last time and holds leading claims today under a 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: 0-3 since hurdle debut win over C&D in October but leading claims today under penalty

runners.name: Changemyluck
runners.spotlight: Improved upon previous form when close, clear second at 40-1 in C&D novice (good to soft) in February, and she would have given the 1-10 favourite even more of a scare had she not swerved left approaching the final flight; she's a contender if backing up that latest performance.
runners.comment: Gave the 1-10 favourite a scare over C&D in February; a contender if backing up that run

runners.name: Kingsman Mail
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on debut in bumper at Ayr (2m, soft) in November; hasn't progressed since but he's from a useful family his trainer has done well with, and he could give a good account on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Hasn't progressed from bumper debut 2nd but could still give good account on hurdle debut

runners.name: Mr Mojo Risin
runners.spotlight: Hint of promise in sole bumper (1m7f, good) and returned from absence to fill fifth of 14 at 40-1 on hurdle debut at Wetherby (2m, soft) in December; wind op since; the Wetherby form reads well and he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Fifth of 14 on hurdle debut at Wetherby and that form reads well; he's one to consider

runners.name: Nobotheratall
runners.spotlight: All three bumper starts on good ground; promising third on first two occasions before making all at Carlisle in October; he brings potential to this hurdle debut and could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Made all in Carlisle bumper in October and brings potential to this hurdle debut

runners.name: Word For Word
runners.spotlight: Promise on one of his three Irish Flat starts and, despite failing to settle and making mistakes, he won in a first-time hood (retained) on his hurdle/stable debut in a juvenile event over C&D (good to soft) in February; tougher task today but he's open to improvement in this code; respected.
runners.comment: Won over C&D on hurdle debut despite making mistakes; open to improvement; respected

runners.name: Bogha Baiste
runners.spotlight: He's run four times and it was his best performance yet when 125-1 third in recent novice hurdle at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft); however, he was beaten 15l and needs another step forward today.
runners.comment: 15l third at 125-1 at Newcastle on second hurdle start; needs another step forward today
runners.owner: A Lee J Matterson D Mullen & Partner

runners.name: Just A Poet
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten when seventh on both starts, most recently her hurdle and stable debut at Catterick (1m7f, good); others are preferred.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten when 7th on both starts, most recently her hurdle/stable debut at Catterick

runners.name: Cavalry Call
runners.spotlight: Two Flat wins but form went the wrong way in that code from last August onwards and he was pulled up at 150-1 on hurdle debut four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Two Flat wins but form went wrong way in that code and he was pulled up on hurdle debut

runners.name: Crokes Cross
runners.spotlight: Down the field last May/June in her two bumpers in Ireland for Edward Power; a transformation is needed on this hurdle/stable debut.
runners.comment: Down the field in both bumpers in Ireland; transformation needed on hurdle/stable debut

runners.name: Ned Broy
runners.spotlight: 0-4 on the Flat with modest form; big odds for both hurdle starts and hasn't shown enough to suggest he's the one to be on today.
runners.comment: Big odds for both hurdle runs; hasn't shown enough to suggest he's the one to be on today

races.name: Racing TV Club Day Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Take A Hike
runners.spotlight: Disappointing on both runs this year and needs to turn things around; however, perhaps first-time cheekpieces will help and she won over C&D (good to soft) in November on her last visit here.
runners.comment: Needs to turn things around but she gets first-time cheekpieces and is a C&D winner

runners.name: Miss Carbo
runners.spotlight: Having been sent off at just 11-4, she won by 5l when upped in trip on last Wednesday's handicap debut at Hereford (2m, good); this unexposed 4yo could be tough to beat under a 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Won by 5l on recent handicap debut at Hereford and could be tough to beat under penalty

runners.name: Ebselysees
runners.spotlight: Good-ground novice hurdle winner who was runner-up in first-time blinkers in Huntingdon handicap (3m1f, good to soft) in November for Noel Williams; however, below par next time and has since changed hands; this inconsistent 7yo goes without headgear on stable debut; others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Runner-up on penultimate start for Noel Williams but below par next time; stable debut

runners.name: Georgies Malt
runners.spotlight: Beaten 12l when fifth of nine at 11-1 on last month's handicap debut at Carlisle (2m1f, soft); improvement is needed but today's forecast better ground could be a positive and she's not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: Fifth on handicap debut at Carlisle; could build on that run now back on better ground

runners.name: Parish Star
runners.spotlight: Won when the hood went back on at Newcastle (2m4f, heavy) in January and followed up at Southwell (2m4f, good to soft); disappointing at Carlisle (2m3f, soft) last time but she was missing the hood, which returns today along with a first-time tongue-tie; could have a part to play.
runners.comment: The return of the hood could be a major plus and she might have a part to play

runners.name: Vanilla Dancer
runners.spotlight: Just one win from her 19 hurdle starts but that came at Catterick (1m7f, soft) in February and she made a bad mistake when fourth at Carlisle (2m1f, soft) next time; she'll be fine now back up in trip and back on better ground, and could give a good account.
runners.comment: Breakthrough hurdles win in February and had an excuse next time; could give good account

runners.name: Am I Bovvered
runners.spotlight: Makes handicap debut off basement mark, which provides her with a first realistic opportunity; watch the betting, but she's shown little at triple-figure odds across her three hurdle starts.
runners.comment: Watch the betting on handicap debut, but she's shown little across her three hurdle starts

runners.name: Theirshegoes
runners.spotlight: Disappointing towards the end of last year but she's 1lb lower than when 1l second over C&D (good) last May and perhaps the first-time cheekpieces will give her a boost; no surprise if she goes well for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Disappointing at end of 2025 but on a handy mark on last May's C&D 2nd; cheekpieces go on

races.name: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap Chase

runners.name: Benefit Ben
runners.spotlight: Won at Musselburgh (2m7f, soft) in December and returned to form at Sedgefield (2m5f, good) a fortnight ago, when he was narrowly in front when falling two out; won over hurdles here last May and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Narrowly in front when falling two out at Sedgefield a fortnight ago; on the shortlist
runners.owner: D & D Armstrong Ltd & L Westwood

runners.name: Divas Doyen
runners.spotlight: A case can be made on form as recent as November but she failed to shine on her two starts in February and needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: A case can be made on form as recent as November but she failed to shine in February

runners.name: The Jad Factor
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 10yo who was pulled up at Newcastle 19 days ago; however, he won here (2m4f, good) last April on the back of being pulled up twice in a row, so it will be no surprise to see him bounce back with a big run.
runners.comment: 10yo who was pulled up at Newcastle but no surprise if this course winner bounces back

runners.name: Nights In Venice
runners.spotlight: Capitalised on a career-low mark to win at Ayr (3m, soft) four weeks ago; he's also effective on good to soft ground and a 4lb rise won't necessarily beat him, but he's inconsistent and has a good record at Ayr, so it's questionable whether he'll produce another good performance today.
runners.comment: Capitalised on career-low mark to win at Ayr; it's questionable whether he'll back that up

runners.name: Poetry Man
runners.spotlight: Won over fences in 2024 and ran okay on his two hurdle starts this season; however, he has a patchy record in chases and was pulled up when reverting to this sphere here in February (reportedly made a respiratory noise).
runners.comment: Patchy record in chases and pulled up when reverting to fences here in February

runners.name: Burgundy Man
runners.spotlight: Running well in defeat over hurdles at Perth last June-September (good/soft) prior to winning on his chase debut there (3m, soft) in October; absent since but he's unexposed over fences and could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Won on chase debut at Perth in October; off since but could have more to offer over fences

runners.name: Looking Splendid
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row over hurdles in October (2m5f/2m4f, good; including here) and ran pretty well when third at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in November; unsuited by soft ground when below par at Musselburgh in December; could have a part to play if taking to fences at the first attempt.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles here in October; could have a part to play if taking to fences

runners.name: Don Occhetti
runners.spotlight: Won at Worcester (2m, good) in August 2024 on final run for Dan Skelton; he's struggled to get competitive this year, following a long absence, but the return to better ground and reapplication of cheekpieces (in which he won at Worcester) could both be positives; he might not be a forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Uncompetitive this year, after a long absence, but better ground/cheekpieces could help

runners.name: Park Rules
runners.spotlight: One to watch in the betting in first-time tongue-tie/cheekpieces on this second start for the stable but there was no return to form from this ex-Irish mare at Sedgefield a fortnight ago, when tailed off.
runners.comment: One to watch in the market but there was no return to form on stable debut a fortnight ago

runners.name: Gorvennec
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a novice hurdle here in November when back from an absence and this is his first run since; however, he showed some ability in novice hurdles in November 2024 and this point winner could improve for the switch to fences; interesting contender on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Tailed off in novice hurdle latest but this point winner now goes chasing for h'cap debut

races.name: Let's Get Ladies Day Ready Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Guet Apens
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat in France, where he stayed well; returned from long absence with encouraging third on hurdle/stable debut at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) in February before 6l win at Carlisle (2m1f, soft) last month; could have potential off his opening mark, especially now up in trip.
runners.comment: Useful on the Flat in France; won on second hurdle start; has potential off opening mark

runners.name: Dedicated Hero
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in the Morebattle Hurdle here in February but he's had wind surgery since and this is a significant drop in grade; he's dropped down the weights and could bounce back with a big run for his in-form yard.
runners.comment: Wind op since pulled up in the Morebattle; he's dropped down the weights for in-form yard

runners.name: Dollar Collar
runners.spotlight: A heavy defeat over fences in November and mid-race fall last month on sole start since, so it's hard to say what sort of form she's in; however, she's just 1lb higher than when winning over hurdles here (2m6f, good) last April and is not written off now back in this discipline.
runners.comment: Hard to say what sort of form she's in but this course winner is not written off today

runners.name: Maillot Blanc
runners.spotlight: Runner-up off this mark over C&D (good to soft) in November; however, this inconsistent 9yo has won just two of his 27 starts and was down the field at Musselburgh later in November when last seen; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D in November but inconsistent and was down the field later that month

runners.name: Feach Amach
runners.spotlight: Won three times last spring (2m4f/2m5f, good/good to soft; including C&D) and followed a break by returning to winning ways with a career best at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft) a fortnight ago, albeit with half the hurdles omitted; could still have more to offer and she's respected up 4lb.
runners.comment: Four wins from her last seven starts and she could still have more to offer; respected

runners.name: Atlantic Lad
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a novice at Sedgefield (2m4f, good to soft) in December when last seen but he's much better than that, having made all in a maiden at Bangor (2m3f, soft) in November; could have a part to play on handicap debut if tapping back into the Bangor promise.
runners.comment: Disappointing in December when last seen but promise previously; makes his handicap debut

runners.name: Sunset Hill
runners.spotlight: She's had a mixed campaign over fences and is now 0-7 in that sphere; however, 3m stretched her at Ayr last time and she reverts to hurdles at a track which brought out the best in her in this sphere last season; chance.
runners.comment: The return to Kelso and drop back in trip could be positives now reverting to hurdles

runners.name: Buy Some Time
runners.spotlight: 50-1 winner of competitive handicap at Punchestown (2m3f, yielding) last April; unplaced on next three starts (May-July) and returns to action having had wind surgery; however, he needs to be at the very top of his game.
runners.comment: Unplaced on last three starts last May-July; wind op since; needs to be at top of his game

races.name: Join Racing TV Now Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lady In The Park
runners.spotlight: Better could have been expected at Carlisle (3m, good to soft) last month but a case can be made on her earlier form, with a win at Perth (3m, good to soft) last April and an encouraging second on her comeback at Carlisle (2m5f, good to soft) in February; not written off.
runners.comment: Better could have been expected at Carlisle last time; a case can be made on earlier form

runners.name: Visual Impact
runners.spotlight: Made the running and returned to form at Ayr (3m, soft) in February, where she was 1l behind in second (clear of the rest) when falling at the final fence; the winner has followed up; could be well treated off a mark just 2lb higher than at Ayr; has shown promise on good to soft ground.
runners.comment: 1l behind a progressive winner when falling at final fence at Ayr; could be well treated

runners.name: Always A Reason
runners.spotlight: Won in January on first two chase starts (2m4f/2m3f, good to soft/soft) and bounced back from a lesser run when second here (2m5f, good to soft) 12 days ago in a first-time tongue-tie (retained); won a 3m point in 2024 and there's every chance he'll stay this far under rules; high on the list.
runners.comment: Progressive 7yo; good second over 2m5f here recently; every chance he'll stay this far
runners.owner: Miss Maria D Myco & John Johnson

runners.name: Cowboy Cooper
runners.spotlight: Won in good style at Ayr (3m, soft) in January and he's also effective on good to soft ground; however, this 10yo has been pulled up/soundly beaten on his last two starts and needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Won at Ayr in January but pulled up/soundly beaten on his two starts since

runners.name: My Lady Elektra
runners.spotlight: 18-race maiden but she was a close fourth on her chase debut over C&D (good) last October when back from a break, then finished second over C&D (soft) in November; there's room for improvement in her jumping and she could have a part to play now back from some more time off.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden but showed promise over C&D last autumn on her first two chase starts

runners.name: Similar Story
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who finished third on her final two outings last season and is well treated on that form; however, she's struggled to get competitive on her seven starts this term and has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Continues to drop down the weights but she's struggled to get competitive this season

runners.name: Kahavari
runners.spotlight: 6yo who is 0-12 since winning on his debut in France in 2024; however, he's finished second over fences on two of his last three starts and the way he kept on at stiff Carlisle (2m5f, good to soft) last month suggests this step up in distance is worth a go; in the mix if seeing out the trip.
runners.comment: Kept on well for head second over stiff 2m5f last time; this longer trip is worth a go

runners.name: Raceview Road
runners.spotlight: He's well handicapped on his 2024 chase form (won here) but his subsequent profile has been mixed and he was soundly beaten when back over fences at Carlisle two months ago; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on 2024 chase form (won here) but his subsequent profile has been mixed

races.name: Racing TV Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Dont Tell Dougie
runners.spotlight: Backed into 9-2 when fourth of six, beaten just over 13l, on recent debut at Doncaster (2m, good to soft); there's potential in his pedigree and he's a possible improver today; interesting to see if there's market support once more.
runners.comment: Fourth of six on recent debut but attracted market support and he's a possible improver

runners.name: Doubly Blessed
runners.spotlight: Easily won an Irish maiden point (3m, good) on debut last May and the runner-up has shown useful form since; bought for Â£65,000 later in May and he's firmly in calculations on this first rules start.
runners.comment: Easily won a point on debut last May and the runner-up has shown useful form since

runners.name: Budworth Boy
runners.spotlight: 8,000euros yearling; fifth foal; dam French 1m3f winner, half-sister to smart/prolific 1m7f-2m3f hurdle/chase winner Master Dino; check the betting, but a watching brief may be the best option on debut.
runners.comment: 8,000euros yearling; check the market but a watching brief may be the best option on debut

runners.name: Jimmy The Jet
runners.spotlight: Â£22,500 3yo; seventh foal; half-brother to winners including Abuffalosoldier (bumper and useful 2m4f-3m3f hurdle/chase), Girl Friday (point/2m3f hurdle; RPR 115) and River Vale (2m hurdle/2m5f chase; 115); this newcomer may be one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Â£22,500 3yo; half-brother to four winners but he may be one for further down the line

runners.name: Berkie
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on debut in an Irish maiden point (3m, good) last May and changed hands for 60,000euros later that month; brings potential to this rules debut and could have a big part to play, especially if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Point runner-up; could have a big part to play, especially if strong in the betting

runners.name: Poets Whisper
runners.spotlight: 16,500euros yearling, 40,000euros 3yo; first foal; dam unraced sister to useful/prolific bumper and 1m7f-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Hatcher, out of unraced half-sister to high-class 2m2f-3m1f hurdle/chase winner China Rock; trainer does very well in bumpers; one to be interested in on debut.
runners.comment: 40,000euros 3yo; trainer does very well in bumpers; one to be interested in on debut

runners.name: Ellistrin Jarla
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m1f hurdle winner for the yard (RPR 91); down the field at 50-1 on last month's debut at Newcastle (good to soft); others preferred.
runners.comment: Down the field at 50-1 on last month's debut at Newcastle (good to soft); others preferred

races.name: BetMGM Supports Safer Gambling Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Zeriya
runners.spotlight: 480,000gns yearling; Too Darn Hot half-sister to two winners, notably Zeyrek (7f-1m4f including Australian Group 3; RPR 118); dam unraced half-sister to 1m4f Group 1 scorer Zarak, out of unbeaten French 1,000 Guineas, French Oaks and Arc winner Zarkava; respected newcomer.
runners.comment: 480,000gns yearling; by Too Darn Hot and from a smart family; respected newcomer

runners.name: Hot Silk
runners.spotlight: Too Darn Hot filly who showed some promise with keeping-on sixth of ten at Yarmouth (1m; 50-1) in sole 2yo start; the form pick, even before factoring in the possibility of improvement.
runners.comment: 50-1 but showed some promise with sixth of ten at Yarmouth; the form pick

runners.name: Palace Artois
runners.spotlight: In excellent hands but recorded another modest RPR switched to AW (8.5f) on reappearance; may be more interesting in handicaps shortly, unless first-time headgear sparks progress.
runners.comment: In good hands but first-time headgear needs to spark progress

runners.name: Romance Spirit
runners.spotlight: 28,000euros foal, 52,000euros yearling; by Teofilo; sibling to two winners, notably Mad Madame Mim (US 5.5f/1m AW); great-granddaughter of 1m4f Group 1 winner; attractively bred newcomer.
runners.comment: 52,000euros yearling; siblings include US AW winner; attractively bred

runners.name: Vitality
runners.spotlight: 48,000gns yearling; New Bay half-sister to six winners, notably Persuasion (7f; RPR 102); dam 1m2f scorer (81); one of three debutantes in the field; market instructive.
runners.comment: 48,000gns yearling; New Bay half-sister to six winners; market helpful
runners.owner: Miss D Finkler A Herd Dr P Holloway 1

runners.name: Silken Bay
runners.spotlight: 11-1, took a good hold in rear and made no headway in 8.5f maiden at Wolverhampton, beating just one rival; however, may settle better and show improvement with hood now fitted.
runners.comment: May settle better and show improvement with hood now fitted

races.name: Make The Move To Midnite Handicap

runners.name: Digital
runners.spotlight: In good form for much of the winter, winning three 5f handicaps, but his form has cooled on his last two starts; others look stronger in this grade.
runners.comment: Three 5f wins this winter but he has gone off the boil on his last two starts; up in class
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Hundred Caps
runners.spotlight: He has not looked back after wind surgery last summer (his second such procedure), recording form figures of 212122 on his last six starts; one of the wins was over C&D and he is greatly respected despite sneaking up the weights.
runners.comment: 1st (incl C&D) or 2nd in all starts after last summer's wind op; should remain competitive

runners.name: Mr Lightside
runners.spotlight: Having tumbled down the weights there were signs of a possible revival when keeping on for third in a 5f handicap at Wolverhampton last week (attracted support); Rossa Ryan retains the ride and today's return to 6f won't be an issue; can't discount.
runners.comment: Last week's Wolverhampton 3rd was more encouraging; not discounted back at 6f

runners.name: Way To Dubai
runners.spotlight: 1m win in January and back to his recent best when a close third at Lingfield last week; runs off the same mark today but he would be more appealing over an extra furlong.
runners.comment: Good 3rd over 7f at Lingfield last week; not sure to benefit from the return to sprinting

runners.name: Dorney Lake
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Michael Bell last April with the second success coming off a 15lb higher mark than today's; struggled for new yard this winter but there were more encouraging signs at Newcastle three weeks ago where he was making up some ground before running into repeated traffic issues; appeals as the type to come good soon but perhaps on turf and in a lower grade.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark and latest run was promising; all wins on turf but he needs monitoring
runners.owner: Mrs C M Clarke Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Cajetan
runners.spotlight: Blew hot and cold for Jack Channon and signs that it may be the same for new connections, shaping well at Wolverhampton in February but turning in lesser efforts twice since; well handicapped if this is one of his good days and the return of cheekpieces (first time for Ruth Carr) looks a plus.
runners.comment: Talented but inconsistent; well handicapped if the return of headgear does the trick

runners.name: Stanley Spencer
runners.spotlight: Went close off this mark over C&D in November; good run at Kempton on his penultimate start and he will find this more manageable than the hot Kempton handicap he contested on Friday; respected.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fair mark on this winter's best; struggled in hot race on Saturday; can revive

runners.name: Kings Crown
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb higher than for his 5f win at Newcastle on New Year's Day; three runs since have not been quite so encouraging though and he may prove vulnerable back up at 6f.
runners.comment: Not on a bad mark on his best form but his last three runs need bettering

runners.name: Palmarian
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in six starts for the Gosdens, including three 7f handicaps last autumn; sold for 16,000gns in October; new trainer drops him back in trip but Cajetan may be her main hope.
runners.comment: Beaten fav in 3 of 6 runs for the Gosdens; sold 16,000gns since latest; back down in trip
runners.owner: MDP Racing And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Musical Touch
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a whimper but he has slipped back down the weights as a consequence; won on last year's reappearance and also ran a fine race after a 175-day layoff in 2024 so unlikely to be lacking in fitness; one to consider.
runners.comment: Goes well fresh and on a good mark on last year's best; one to consider
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

races.name: Bet Â£10 Get Â£40 With BetMGM Handicap

runners.name: Haddiah
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who is from a family that has served her owner well; faded into modest fourth over C&D on reappearance but looks open to improvement now handicapping with the outing under her belt and tongue-tie fitted; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Open to improvement now handicapping with C&D reappearance under her belt; interesting

runners.name: Hardstyle
runners.spotlight: Campaigned exclusively on AW; strike-rate of 3-7 since handicapping, the most recent win at Wolverhampton (8.5f) on penultimate start; creditable third at Kempton (7f) latest; remains 18lb higher than opening mark, but in the mix.
runners.comment: Record of 3-7 in handicaps; ran creditably last time; in the mix

runners.name: Tewkesbury
runners.spotlight: Solid four-race maiden, having not yet finished out of the frame; most recently went close in Chelmsford handicap (1m) on reappearance and looks capable of winning a contest of this nature; bred to do better still, being closely related to a useful performer for his connections.
runners.comment: Went close in Chelmsford handicap on reappearance; bred to do better still; big player

runners.name: Ebn Sabt
runners.spotlight: Record is 1-9, the win at Lingfield (1m) in February when wearing first-time blinkers; has somewhat regressed in the retained blinds since and can be opposed in another race that features several less exposed rivals.
runners.comment: Has somewhat regressed since winning at Lingfield in first-time blinkers

runners.name: Booziebrunch
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since well held (faced stiff task) in 7f sales race six months ago; two attempts over 1m comprise a win at Chelmsford and very close second at Lingfield last August, during his spell in blinkers; removal of the headgear presents a dilemma.
runners.comment: Good performances in two attempts over 1m on AW; removal of blinkers presents a dilemma

runners.name: Whats The Plan
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to two stablemates who have a combined record of 8-9 on AW; duly gained his maiden success in this sphere; rather disappointing on final 2yo start (handicap debut; gelded since) but may still have more to offer; not written off.
runners.comment: Gained his 2yo win on AW and pedigree suggests he should still have more to offer

runners.name: Folk Pageant
runners.spotlight: Second over 7f at Ayr in sole 2yo start (form well advertised by the useful winner since); went one better, having again started favourite, at Lingfield (1m, AW; made all) on reappearance; this nicely bred filly looks the type to progress and do well in handicaps; respected.
runners.comment: Form of 2yo effort is strong; made all at Lingfield on reappearance; commands respect
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Boy Named Sioux
runners.spotlight: Went too freely, despite wearing first-time hood, when bidding for a November double over 7f on AW; returns to the scene of his win but needs to settle better in the retained headgear, especially upped to 1m.
runners.comment: Returns to the scene of his win but needs to settle better upped in distance
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: On The Inlet
runners.spotlight: Took a good hold in three runs (7f) last term but the combination of a gelding operation and being more mature this year may result in an ability to settle; improvement, which is necessary, therefore looks possible.
runners.comment: May settle better this year; improvement, which is necessary, therefore looks possible

races.name: Midnite A Next Generation Betting App Handicap

runners.name: Isla Bella
runners.spotlight: She ran her best race for a while when second at Wolverhampton last week (5f; cheekpieces on for the first time); ahead of the handicapper as a consequence but others look more appealing for win purposes.
runners.comment: Ran well in new cheekpieces last week; ahead of handicapper but may still prove vulnerable

runners.name: Fortunate Star
runners.spotlight: Won classified events over C&D and at Newcastle (penalised) before being brushed aside by Little Mi Mi at Newcastle three weeks ago; a 5lb turnaround in the weights should see things finish closer today.
runners.comment: Found Little Mi Mi too strong in his hat-trick bid last month; 5lb better off this time

runners.name: Little Mi Mi
runners.spotlight: Shed her maiden tag on New Year's Day in a 5f handicap at Newcastle and she advanced her form when readily accounting for Fortunate Star back there three weeks ago; 6lb higher mark and the sharper track demand another step up the ladder.
runners.comment: New personal best with smooth Newcastle win last month; sharper track/6lb rise to overcome

runners.name: Okami
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins at Newcastle this winter and he finished third off a 2lb higher mark back there in February; disappointed behind Little Mi Mi last month; could rebound with a promising 7lb claimer booked.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins at Newcastle this winter; below par latest; promising 7lb claimer booked

runners.name: Auntie Jo
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 19th attempt (first in a visor) when smoothly landing a 5f classified event at Newcastle three weeks ago; up in the weights but she has been competitive off higher marks before; one to consider.
runners.comment: Easy win in 5f classified event at Newcastle last month (visored first time); solid claims

runners.name: Lets Go Hugo
runners.spotlight: Finished behind Little Mi Mi and Fortunate Star when fourth at Newcastle three weeks ago; that was after a 131-day break so he could take a step forward; others are still preferred for win purposes
runners.comment: Finished behind 2 of today's rivals at Newcastle last month; sharper today but needs more

runners.name: Master Zack
runners.spotlight: Landed a gamble over C&D 12 months ago (in this combination of accessories for the first time); ended 2025 quietly and he was well beaten on last month's return to action (no accessories); has plenty to prove but it wouldn't be a total bolt from the blue were he to revive.
runners.comment: Landed a gamble over C&D last April; sharper for return and accessories are back on; risky

runners.name: Silky Robin
runners.spotlight: 0-18 on AW and can get it wrong at the start; on the plus side, he is dropping into a 0-60 today and you don't have to go back too far to find form that gives him a strong chance.
runners.comment: 0-18 on AW but slips into a 0-60 today so a revival wouldn't be a huge shock

runners.name: Arlington
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer with three C&D wins on his record, including on his penultimate start in January; not so good behind Okami five days later; has tended to come on for the run after breaks in the past.
runners.comment: Conditions to suit but not at his best last time and he has a mixed record after a break

runners.name: Colors Of Freedom
runners.spotlight: Hard to knock on the consistency front and Saturday's third over C&D (visored first time) was another solid effort; set to run her race once again but it might not be enough to end a losing run which stands at 21.
runners.comment: Consistent mare; good C&D third on Saturday; needs more to end losing run of 21

runners.name: Street Life
runners.spotlight: Veteran who was recording the 14th win of his career when scoring off this mark over C&D in January; short of his best on his last four starts but the ability is there if his new rider can coax it out of him.
runners.comment: Prolific winner, including C&D, and on a winning mark; new rider has had a fine winter

runners.name: Opal Storm
runners.spotlight: Yet to shine on AW but she has slipped to a tempting mark and last month's return to action (6f) should have blown away any cobwebs; the return to 5f is in her favour too and she could run a big race at a price.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark and last month's run should have sharpened her up; drop to 5f a plus

runners.name: Jeany May
runners.spotlight: 0-9 in 2025 but she hit the frame on a few occasions, including her seasonal return; makes her comeback this spring off a reduced mark and she has run well over C&D in the past; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Ran well fresh last spring and she's fully effective over C&D; returns on a dangerous mark

races.name: Win Â£250,000 With BetMGM's Golden Goals Handicap

runners.name: Espanita
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of her debut second at Kempton in February (7f) by readily making all at Wolverhampton last month; hard to believe she'll enjoy quite such a soft time of things up front today but she moves into handicaps with potential.
runners.comment: Second & first in two 7f runs this year; open to improvement and she's a strong candidate

runners.name: Homestrait
runners.spotlight: She finished second in her first four starts before making the breakthrough in a Doncaster maiden (7f, good) last July; added a 7f seller at Musselburgh one month later but she ended 2025 with two heavy defeats; this is just her second handicap run but she was beaten off a lower mark on her first.
runners.comment: Two 7f wins as a 2yo but ended 2025 with two heavy defeats; vulnerable to improvers

runners.name: Keep An Eye On It
runners.spotlight: Two encouraging efforts at Redcar (6f, good) last autumn; 5f proved inadequate on his reappearance five weeks ago but he stayed on quite nicely under a hand ride; looks a likely improver stepping up to 7f for his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Two promising 6f runs as a 2yo; outpaced over 5f on return; more to come now handicapping

runners.name: Sporting Light
runners.spotlight: He had been banging at the door prior to his win in a 7f nursery at Wolverhampton last August; two disappointing runs followed, after which he was gelded, and he returns from a six-month break with a point to prove.
runners.comment: 7f win at Wolverhampton last summer; two lesser runs followed; gelded; has point to prove
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Tekitoff
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on soft ground in two starts for Danny Brooke last summer; marked improvement on his stable/AW debut three weeks ago when fourth of nine in a 7f maiden at Newcastle but they went slowly to halfway and he was well positioned; opening mark looks tough but he is at least unexposed.
runners.comment: Improved on stable/AW debut when 4th last month but was well positioned in slowly run race

runners.name: Everydaysasaturday
runners.spotlight: Modest form in five runs over sprint trips; attracted some support at Newcastle on his handicap debut/reappearance 12 days ago but couldn't land a telling blow; improvement required for the step up in trip.
runners.comment: Modest form in five starts, including recent handicap debut; now moves up in trip

runners.name: Polka Blue
runners.spotlight: Ran to a fair level in her two AW starts as a 2yo, including when third over C&D in October; not obviously thrown in for this handicap debut but she is unexposed at 7f and should have more to offer.
runners.comment: Third over C&D in her one run at 7f; makes her handicap debut at a realistic level; chance

races.name: Midnite Are Upping The Betting Game Handicap

runners.name: Captain Robert
runners.spotlight: Broadly sound AW form since October features an account-opening win over 1m6f; posted a solid-looking effort in new headgear (blinkers) over C&D most recently; runs off the same mark and Conor Whiteley takes off a bonus 7b; highly respected.
runners.comment: Broadly sound AW form since October; solid third over C&D most recently; highly respected

runners.name: Zephlyn
runners.spotlight: Has done all his winning at 2m; showed clear signs of a return to form with third-place finish over 1m6f at Southwell last time; possibilities off a handy mark returned to his optimum trip.
runners.comment: Clear signs of a return to form over 1m6f at Southwell last time; all wins over 2m

runners.name: A Mere Bagatelle
runners.spotlight: Four wins over jumps for previous yard; 0-7 (Flat/hurdles) for current stable but remains in form and has a fighting chance off current mark provided he takes well to the surface on belated AW debut.
runners.comment: 0-7 (Flat/hurdles) for current yard but has a fighting chance provided he takes to AW

runners.name: Bruce Banner
runners.spotlight: AW/hurdles winner; ran well in first-time visor at Newcastle (12.5f) returned to this code most recently; place possibilities in the retained headgear, provided he's as effective back up in distance.
runners.comment: Place possibilities in the retained visor, provided he's as effective back up in trip

runners.name: Charlie Darling
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner in Ireland; not solid on his two AW efforts for new stable since a lengthy layoff; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish 6yo who is not solid on his two efforts for new stable

runners.name: Rogue Justice
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over hurdles last time; has taken Flat form to a new level since switched to AW, finishing second at Wolverhampton (1m6f) last September then going one better at Chelmsford (2m) in January; open to further progress back in this sphere, provided he takes to new headgear.
runners.comment: Won at Chelmsford in most recent AW attempt and may be capable of further progress

runners.name: Payment Plan
runners.spotlight: Two from two over C&D, namely wins in November 2024 during a good spell on AW; eventually lost his form and has been absent since last May but looks interesting back here.
runners.comment: Absent since last May but looks interesting back here, being 2-2 over C&D

runners.name: Haaland
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins for previous stable; 0-8 for current yard and his form has declined rather sharply; however, he may produce a resurgent display back at this level, being 2-2 in Class 6.
runners.comment: Form has declined but he may prove resurgent back at this level, being 2-2 in Class 6

races.name: Create Your Bet Builders At Midnite Handicap

runners.name: Hackney Diamonds
runners.spotlight: Form at Southwell features two wins (1m3f/1m4f) during the winter and third-place finishes in last two outings; receives weight all round and holds a solid chance kept to this track.
runners.comment: Suited by Southwell, remains in form and receives weight all round; solid chance

runners.name: Phaedra
runners.spotlight: Couple of starts at about 1m for new yard, going close at Southwell then never landing a blow at Wolverhampton; interesting back up in trip, having done her winning at about 1m4f.
runners.comment: Interesting back up in trip on third start for new stable

runners.name: Spec Of Light
runners.spotlight: Gained sole success for previous yard at Southwell (1m); ran creditably, finishing half-length second to Chicago Storm, over C&D (on debut for new stable) most recently; in the mix.
runners.comment: Half-length second to Chicago Storm over C&D on stable debut; in the mix

runners.name: Chicago Storm
runners.spotlight: Steps back up in grade but looks shortlist material, having registered a C&D win under Millie Wonnacott most recently, taking well to Southwell at the first attempt and taking AW record to 2-7.
runners.comment: Steps back up in grade but is a likely player, having scored over C&D last time

runners.name: Pigeon House
runners.spotlight: Claimed out of Jennie Candlish's yard for Â£12,000 after winning over hurdles at Catterick last time; could go well but looks no certainty to follow up, being 0-12 on the Flat (mostly turf starts).
runners.comment: Claimed after winning over hurdles last time; 0-12 on the Flat

runners.name: Solanna
runners.spotlight: Prevailed narrowly under Rossa Ryan at Kempton (1m3f) last time, taking AW strike-rate to 7-31; likely to remain competitive back up just 2lb, provided he takes to Southwell (first run here).
runners.comment: Prevailed narrowly under Rossa Ryan at Kempton last time; back up only 2lb

runners.name: Latin
runners.spotlight: Responded well to new headgear at Lingfield (1m2f) last week, getting up in the final strides and taking AW record to 5-24; incurs 5lb penalty but warrants respect in the retained hood.
runners.comment: Incurs a 5lb penalty for Lingfield win but warrants respect in the retained hood

runners.name: Wood Whisperer
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; shaped with some promise, beaten about 3l amid traffic issues, at Kempton (1m3f) on stable/seasonal debut; interesting off 2lb lower assuming he builds on that effort.
runners.comment: Shaped with some promise at Kempton on stable/seasonal debut; interesting

runners.name: Theophilos
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 7yo who brings a long losing spell to this debut for new yard, as it's three years since his sole win; finished second in a Dundalk claimer (1m4f) when last seen.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish 7yo who brings a long losing spell to this debut for new yard

races.name: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Chase

runners.name: Maxxum
runners.spotlight: Better known as a hurdler, with seven wins in that sphere up to 3m; was below form at Leopardstown last time in first-time cheekpieces; did win on chasing debut in 2023 but was pulled up on last start in this sphere in 2024; however, is seemingly the choice of Jack Kennedy so commands maximum respect.
runners.comment: Better known as a hurdler but the choice of Jack Kennedy so commands maximum respect

runners.name: Dee Capo
runners.spotlight: Won for the second time over fences when taking a Punchestown handicap on return last November; fourth in a valuable Leopardstown handicap since but was below form at Cheltenham last time; seems to be the yard's second string on riding arrangements but still respected.
runners.comment: Below form last time; seems yard's second string on riding arrangements; still respected

runners.name: Look To The West
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner was placed in Grade 3 company in that sphere; got off the mark over fences on second try when winning at Kilbeggan last year; tailed off when fell on return at Fairyhouse; questions to answer in this company.
runners.comment: Tailed off when fell on return at Fairyhouse; questions to answer in this company

runners.name: Mount Frisco
runners.spotlight: Won over fences at Gowran and hurdles here in February but was below form over fences at Limerick since; has a lot to find against some of these rivals; due to run in the 6.42 at Limerick on Tuesday.
runners.comment: Below form over fences at Limerick last time; has a lot to find against some of these

runners.name: Along The Way
runners.spotlight: Maiden over hurdles and fences is out of his depth in this company; handicaps will suit better in time.
runners.comment: Maiden over hurdles and fences is out of his depth; handicaps will suit better

races.name: KEP Italia Handicap Chase

runners.name: Wyoming Baby
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; hit the frame in pair of handicap chases last season before off the mark over hurdles at Cork (24.5f, good) last May; pulled up on Thurles chase return last month; ground concerns.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles on good at Cork last season; pulled up on chase return; ground concerns

runners.name: Mount Shenshan
runners.spotlight: Twice placed over fences in October/November; conditions very testing when pulled up in course handicap hurdle in February; much-improved when fourth of 14 in C&D handicap (soft) last week, doing best work late; contender from same mark.
runners.comment: Fourth in C&D handicap (soft) last week, doing best work late; contender from same mark

runners.name: Snow Punt
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner is 0-14 under rules but standout run on latest when beaten a nose at Limerick (2m7f, heavy), running on gamely to the line; 4lb higher now but every chance with a repeat.
runners.comment: Yet to win under rules but only beaten a nose at Limerick last time; every chance here

runners.name: Carlas Big Jim
runners.spotlight: First reserve; dual-winning hurdler is 0-11 over fences; has generally struggled this term; 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: Dual-winning hurdler is 0-11 over fences; has generally struggled this term; reserve

runners.name: Without Exception
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; hurdles winner placed twice from five starts this season; struggled in two attempts chasing previously; chance at his best but 4lb wrong.
runners.comment: Placed twice from five hurdle starts this term; struggled in two attempts chasing; reserve

runners.name: Love Like This
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; out of form lately and is now 0-39 under rules; makes limited appeal; 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: Out of form lately and is now 0-39 under rules; makes limited appeal; reserve

runners.name: Long Road
runners.spotlight: Consecutive unseats in maiden hunter chases but got round to finish third in Thurles handicap (21.5f, soft) last month, albeit 15l behind the winner; given a forceful ride when fourth at Wexford (19.5f, soft) 12 days later; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Two solid runs from the front of late; likely to give it a good go again; each-way chance

runners.name: The Rebel County
runners.spotlight: Winner of a novice hunter chase last spring on good to yielding; subsequent form fails to inspire; distant sixth of 15 in Thurles handicap four weeks ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won novice hunter chase last spring on good to yielding; subsequent form fails to inspire

runners.name: Rusheen
runners.spotlight: Unseated at Naas five out in February before pulling up at Thurles last month; had previously won well at Naas on seasonal/stable debut; 7lb higher now; bounce back required.
runners.comment: Won well at Naas on seasonal/stable debut; regressive since; bounce back required

runners.name: Roc My Haarth
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler with uneven form; definite player judged on runner-up finishes at Limerick and Punchestown this season; the form of the latter has worked out well as the third Live To Laugh has won twice since; ran below market expectations at Limerick three weeks ago but big player if he takes to fences.
runners.comment: Form of penultimate run has worked out well; ran below best at Limerick; chase debut

runners.name: Baldurs Gate
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in 3m1f Punchestown handicap in late 2023; kept busy in recent weeks, standout on latest when third of seven at Limerick (24.5f, soft to heavy) 11 days ago; booking of Darragh O'Keeffe a plus.
runners.comment: No win since 2023; third of seven at Limerick 11 days ago; booking of O'Keeffe a plus

runners.name: Made In The Woods
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over fences at Limerick in 2023 off 88 (3m, soft); placed a couple of times since; fair sixth of 16 here (19.5f, heavy) on first start for Mick Winters in December but well beaten at Navan; back with former yard; market will guide.
runners.comment: No win since 2023; fair effort for Mick Winters here in December; back with former yard

runners.name: North Of Nashville
runners.spotlight: Won over 3m1f at Wexford in 2023; ran only once in 2024; last of three in Lisronagh point-to-point in November; eighth in better-quality contest at Punchestown in December; weakened quickly when pulled up at Navan in January; chance at her best.
runners.comment: Won at Wexford in 2023; glimmer of hope from Punchestown run in December; pulled up latest

runners.name: Is Charlie Around
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent maiden went close in handicap hurdles this term; fell two out here in 2m handicap when still in the mix in February; narrowly beaten by well-backed Charles Byrnes runner in 2m beginners' chase here last week; stamina to prove on handicap chase bow.
runners.comment: Narrowly beaten by well-backed Charles Byrnes runner in 2m beginners' chase here last week

runners.name: My Cullen
runners.spotlight: First run for Shark Hanlon after leaving Henrietta Knight; gave a good account when second at Lingfield (2m7f, good to soft) in November from 2lb higher; failed to fire in two outings since; this ground may be too deep for him.
runners.comment: Failed to fire in two outings since Lingfield 2nd; this ground may be too deep; new yard

runners.name: Rhythmic Tune
runners.spotlight: Gained sole hurdles win in Limerick handicap (2m5f, yielding) in October on just his seventh career start; didn't handle the mud there next time; dam from the family of See More Business so market can guide on chase debut.
runners.comment: One hurdles win but didn't enjoy the mud last time; market can guide on chase debut

runners.name: Reve Inoui
runners.spotlight: Pulled up at Fairyhouse in February on first run in 644 days; pulled up again at Cork two weeks ago; point-to-point winner needs to improve on chase bow.
runners.comment: Pulled up twice since returning from long absence; point winner; chase bow

races.name: KEP Italia Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Fabhcun
runners.spotlight: Finished a modest fifth on Gowran debut, beaten 7l; went backwards next time at Fairyhouse; will need to improve plenty but that's not impossible on just his third start for top stable stepping up in trip.
runners.comment: Will need to improve plenty but that's not impossible on just his third start

runners.name: Kaiser De Chanay
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point-to-point; sets the standard judged on his second run under rules when fifth at Navan (tongue-tied), a race that has thrown up three subsequent winners; however, has a bit to prove after tailing off over C&D but the tongue-tie was left off that day (back on now).
runners.comment: Repeat of Navan run could be enough but was poor over C&D when last seen

runners.name: Drombane
runners.spotlight: Nicely bred but disappointed on bumper debut at Punchestown, weakening quickly; completely tailed off next time at Thurles; Paul Townend booked for hurdles debut but big leap of faith required; tongue-tied.
runners.comment: Paul Townend booked for hurdles debut but big leap of faith required; tongue-tied

runners.name: Courageous Cleo
runners.spotlight: Second runner for Gordon Elliott; made little impact in a bumper and two tries hurdling; could be one for handicaps; Fabhcun has stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Second runner for Elliott; could be one for handicaps; Fabhcun has stronger credentials

runners.name: Lunar Majesty
runners.spotlight: Needs to jump more fluently but outran 300-1 odds when sixth of 16 over C&D in February, finishing just ahead of Kaiser De Chanay; will need to improve again but has place claims.
runners.comment: Sixth of 16 over C&D in February, finishing just ahead of Kaiser De Chanay; place claims

runners.name: Keep Em Quite
runners.spotlight: Placed in point-to-points; opening hurdles run at Leopardstown not devoid of promise but regressed at Down Royal; market will reveal expectations.
runners.comment: Opening hurdles run at Leopardstown not devoid of promise but regressed at Down Royal

runners.name: Lord Of The Locke
runners.spotlight: Sixth of 17 in valuable sales bumper at Fairyhouse last April; beaten favourite at Limerick on hurdles debut; not disgraced behind El Cairos at Thurles in January; fair fifth of 16 at Punchestown; considered in a contest lacking depth.
runners.comment: Has bits and pieces of form, including when behind El Cairos at Thurles; not discounted

runners.name: Shine Bright
runners.spotlight: Struggled in a bumper in 2024; same story in two hurdle outings; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Struggled in a bumper in 2024; same story in two hurdle outings; hard to fancy

runners.name: Lord De Choisel
runners.spotlight: Difficult to see him featuring on the evidence of his runs at Leopardstown and Limerick; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Difficult to see him featuring on the evidence of his runs at Leopardstown and Limerick

runners.name: Wildhorserider
runners.spotlight: Distant second in a maiden point-to-point; triple-figure odds and tailed off in two hurdle outings.
runners.comment: Distant second in a maiden point; triple-figure odds and tailed off in two hurdle outings

runners.name: Sams Favourite
runners.spotlight: Refused at the second flight on debut at Leopardstown; never a factor when 200-1 at Cork a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Refused at the second flight on debut at Leopardstown; never a factor when 200-1 at Cork

runners.name: Deebert
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; no sign of winning potential over hurdles; nasty fall two out at Limerick before better effort when seventh of 14 in 16.5f course maiden; much more needed.
runners.comment: Better effort when seventh of 14 in 16.5f course maiden but much more needed

races.name: KEP Italia Superior Helmets Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: One To Witness
runners.spotlight: Showed promise on debut in a Naas bumper when beaten 11l by a subsequent Listed winner; no time wasted in going over hurdles and commands plenty of respect as his powerful yard's first string.
runners.comment: Promise on bumper debut; commands plenty of respect as his powerful yard's first string

runners.name: Celtic Alliance
runners.spotlight: Cost 40,000euros as a foal; half-sister to winners Absolute Notions (bumper and useful 2m3f/2m4f hurdle) and Colleen Danu (2m4f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to high-class 2m4f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Macgeorge; decent pedigree and can run well but One To Witness looks the yard's main chance.
runners.comment: Decent pedigree and can run well but One To Witness looks the yard's main chance
runners.owner: Mrs Judith Matthews & Mrs Sheila ORyan

runners.name: Blossom Trixie
runners.spotlight: Well held in two maiden hurdles and will have to improve loads even though this looks a weaker race.
runners.comment: Well held in two maiden hurdles and will have to improve loads even in this weaker race

runners.name: Dooks
