runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; brother to smart 2m/2m1f hurdle/chase winner Amarillo Sky, closely related to 2m hurdle winner Leahs Tricks; dam unraced sister to useful 2m3f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Ashtown Lad; well-bred sort cost 52,000euros as a 3yo but seems to be the yard's second string on debut.
runners.comment: Cost 52,000euros as a 3yo but seems to be the yard's second string on debut

runners.name: Mastertown Miss
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to winners Lowry's Bar (useful 1m7f-2m3f hurdle/chase), Getaway Goldie (2m4f hurdle-3m chase) and Glentruan (2m4f hurdle); dam point winner/placed 2m hurdles; nicely-bred filly cost 15,500euros as a 3yo; capable of a decent showing on debut and worth a market check.
runners.comment: Nicely-bred filly should be capable of a decent showing on debut and worth a market check

runners.name: Haydens Star
runners.spotlight: Second foal; unraced dam out of 2m3f chase/2m4f hurdle winner; likely best watched on debut for future reference.
runners.comment: Likely best watched on debut for future reference

runners.name: Mac Dara
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; half-brother to point winners Oaks Empress and Champagne Pop; dam bumper and 2m1f/2m4f hurdle winner, half-sister to two hurdle winners; likely best watched on debut for future reference.
runners.comment: Second runner for yard; likely best watched on debut for future reference

races.name: KEP Italia Mares Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Liss Eile
runners.spotlight: Some decent bumper runs without winning and the best of three maiden hurdle efforts came on debut at Tramore last August; poor the last twice and might need better ground.
runners.comment: Best maiden hurdle effort came at Tramore last August; poor since; may need better ground

runners.name: Finnicky Filly
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark when winning over C&D in December on testing ground; pulled up twice here since but better the last twice; hard to have too much faith in.
runners.comment: C&D winner pulled up twice here since but better the last twice; can't have much faith in

runners.name: Wild Wild Wind
runners.spotlight: Has posted several decent efforts in defeat over the winter including over C&D; was below form at Cork last time but this softer ground should suit better.
runners.comment: Has run well here; below form at Cork last time but this softer ground should suit better

runners.name: Ballinearla Girl
runners.spotlight: Maiden has posted some good runs in bumpers and over hurdles but has been well beaten on last four starts and might be better on a sound surface; tongue-tie and cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Well beaten last four starts; may be better on a sound surface; tongue-tie and cheekpieces
runners.owner: Eoghan Michael Wallis & Gerard Fintan Wallis

runners.name: Midnight Eagle
runners.spotlight: Won at Limerick over this trip last year off 4lb lower; hit and miss since and has been below form the last twice so needs a return to her best.
runners.comment: Has been below form the last twice so needs a return to her best

runners.name: Ellies Rock
runners.spotlight: Was second to a well treated rival at Punchestown in January but was below that form back at the same track since; needs best down in trip.
runners.comment: Second to a well treated rival at Punchestown in January but was below that form since

runners.name: Volez Vous
runners.spotlight: Won consecutive races over hurdles at this track last year, including over C&D; was poor in three chases and one hurdle start over the winter; has to return to best for a new yard.
runners.comment: C&D winner; poor in three chases and one hurdle start over the winter; new yard

runners.name: Dolly Watkins
runners.spotlight: Reserve; posted a solid effort back from an absence at Down Royal when just denied; raised 4lb and defied that rise when winning the 5.42 at Limerick Tuesday; big player if turning out again under a 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Won at Limerick Tuesday and will be a big player if turned out again; reserve

runners.name: Cry Before Dawn
runners.spotlight: Best run when third over C&D on handicap debut in February but was below that form at Naas since so has to rebound.
runners.comment: Third over C&D on handicap debut but was below that form at Naas since so has to rebound

runners.name: Ode To Joy
runners.spotlight: Beaten 6l at Naas over this trip on handicap debut but was keen over 2m3f here since while still running well; the drop back to 2m should suit and is on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Keen over 2m3f here last time while still running well; the drop back to 2m should suit

runners.name: Bearami Creek
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m3f at Navan in September and decent efforts in defeat at Listowel and Punchestown since; can be a player if ready enough after a break.
runners.comment: Was in decent form last autumn; can be a player if ready enough after a break

runners.name: Might Be The One
runners.spotlight: Just denied at Limerick in December over 2m3f and best run since was when beaten 10l over this trip at Naas last time; needs a bit more but a player.
runners.comment: Beaten 10l over this trip at Naas last time; needs a bit more but a player

runners.name: Evelyn
runners.spotlight: Has posted some decent efforts without winning in bumpers and maiden hurdles; was out of her depth in Listed company last time; this is more suitable but needs her best.
runners.comment: Out of her depth in Listed company last time; this more suitable but needs her best

runners.name: Powerful Rosie
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden has been well beaten in three maiden hurdles and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden has been well beaten in three maiden hurdles; others make more appeal

runners.name: Graces Legacy
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three maiden hurdles and isn't easy to fancy on handicap debut for all that this is a drop in class.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three maiden hurdles and isn't easy to fancy on handicap debut

runners.name: Question Asked
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in four maiden hurdles and isn't easy to fancy on handicap debut for all that this is a drop in class.
runners.comment: Tailed-off in four maiden hurdles and isn't easy to fancy on handicap debut

runners.name: Concert Party
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner in Britain; down the field in three maiden hurdles for this yard but is likely to do better in this grade and market strength could indicate a prominent showing.
runners.comment: Down the field in three maiden hurdles for this yard; likely to do better in this grade

races.name: KEP Italia Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Tingarran Express
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner; battled well from the front to land mares' handicap at Tramore last August (16.5f, good); looked in need of the run on course return in February; likely to strip much fitter now; won here on her second start after a break around this time last season; major contender.
runners.comment: Dual course winner looked in need of the run here on return in February; contender

runners.name: Our Uncle Jack
runners.spotlight: Adoption of front-running tactics have had a transformative effect; 23l all the way win off 91 here (2m1f, heavy) followed by another dominant win at Fairyhouse (2m, heavy) off 12lb higher; handicapper possibly caught up when pulled up at Fairyhouse (2m4f) but drop in trip should help.
runners.comment: Two impressive wins from the front before pulled up at Fairyhouse; drop in trip a help

runners.name: Old Kenmare
runners.spotlight: Improved for a switch to course handicaps last season, a good third (18.5f, good) followed by 2l second of 11 (2m1f, good to yielding) in April; still had plenty to do when a faller four out on Cork return last month; unproven in the mud.
runners.comment: Improved for a switch to course handicaps last season; unproven in the mud

runners.name: Gino Bella
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner in November; in good form for this yard since, placing in three of her four starts, headed in the dying strides when second of 22 at Cork a fortnight ago (2m1f, yielding); her worst run of late was at this track.
runners.comment: Hit the frame three times for this yard recently but her worst recent effort was here

runners.name: Palace Boy
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner for Fergal O'Brien and ran in the 2023 Supreme Novices'; three-time Flat winner; back from long absence when tailed off on sole Flat outing for Barry Brennan in December; bought for just Â£1,400 in January; Irish/handicap hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner; hurdles winner ran in 2023 Supreme; cheaply bought in January

runners.name: Crohan Lady
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; improved for a switch to handicaps in February when taking a close third at Thurles (2m1f, soft to heavy); midfield at Limerick off 3lb higher 11 days ago on debut for new yard; in the mix.
runners.comment: Close third at Thurles before midfield at Limerick off 3lb higher 11 days ago; in the mix

runners.name: Keiths Boy
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo shaped with some promise on January's handicap debut at Naas, weaking late over 2m3f; not at the same level there on latest; each-way contender if at his best.
runners.comment: Some promise taken from handicap debut run at Naas in January; each-way player

runners.name: Marlpark
runners.spotlight: Generally weak form since course maiden win in October 2024 but step back in the right direction last week when runner-up in course handicap (2m3f, soft), no match for the winner; chance from unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Runner-up in course handicap latest, no match for the winner; chance from unchanged mark

runners.name: Market Ready
runners.spotlight: Fair fourth in C&D maiden on debut a year ago; out of his depth in Punchestown festival bumper following month; creditable third in Cork maiden in October; should be sharper after a run in a Gowran maiden last month; definite chance in first handicap.
runners.comment: Should be sharper after a run in Gowran maiden; definite chance in first handicap

runners.name: Blue Train
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner in Britain; some promise on hurdle/stable debut when fourth at Fairyhouse, raced keenly; made little impact at Thurles and Naas but could be a different proposition on handicap hurdle debut for shrewd connections; monitor the betting.
runners.comment: Could be a different proposition on handicap hurdle debut for shrewd connections

runners.name: Elbow Lane
runners.spotlight: Third in a point-to-point; tailed off in three maiden hurdles; dangerous to discount on handicap debut given the source.
runners.comment: Tailed off in 3 maiden hurdles; dangerous to discount on handicap debut given the source

runners.name: Wise Man
runners.spotlight: Placed in two small-field point-to-points; set some stiff tasks in three maiden hurdles; the market should prove informative on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Some stiff tasks in 3 maiden hurdles; the market should prove informative on handicap bow

runners.name: Silent Selector
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in Limerick handicap last April (2m3f, good); failed to complete next three starts before narrowly scoring at Tramore in August (20.5f, good); jumping issues evident at Tramore when last seen; prefers better ground.
runners.comment: Both wins have been on good ground; jumping issues can be evident; wants better ground

runners.name: Stay In The Game
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up on the Flat in Britain; has managed a couple of decent efforts over hurdles; made no impression on the all-weather at Dundalk when last seen; market will guide on handicap hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Couple of decent efforts over hurdles; tailed off at Dundalk last time; market will guide

races.name: Happy Easter To All Our Patrons INH Flat Race

runners.name: Game Theatre
runners.spotlight: Leading Light gelding cost 16,000euros at three; half-brother to three winners; dam point winner; won second point-to-point start at Turtulla for Paul Power in January; contender for top yard.
runners.comment: Won second point start at Turtulla for Paul Power in January; contender for top yard

runners.name: Rememberhesmine
runners.spotlight: Best run in six bumper starts came when runner-up at Limerick over Christmas time in 2023; has been off the track close to two years; market will reveal expectations on return.
runners.comment: Fair bumper form; off track close to 2 years; market will reveal expectations on return

runners.name: Bridgie Browne
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam placed in point, half-sister to smart 2m hurdle/chase winner River Liane, out of French 1m1f 2yo winner; stable 1-27 in bumpers.
runners.comment: Dam half-sister to smart 2m hurdle/chase winner River Liane; stable 1-27 in bumpers

runners.name: High Kick Kev
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to point winner Port And Brandy; unraced dam out of 1m7f Flat/3m hurdle winning sister to dam of Stayers' Hurdle runner-up Ronald Pump; picked up for 60,000euros after easily winning a Tallow point-to-point in February; yard 3-6 in bumpers this season.
runners.comment: Won a point cosily in January; bought for 60,000euros; yard 3-6 in bumpers this season

runners.name: Little John
runners.spotlight: Dam bumper and 2m2f/2m5f hurdle winner, closely related to high-class winner Champ, out of half-sister to triple Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Best Mate; made little impact in three point-to-points; best watched.
runners.comment: Staying pedigree; made little impact in three point-to-points; best watched

runners.name: World As One
runners.spotlight: Waldgeist gelding cost 82,000euros at three; closely related to a winner, half-brother to eight more inc Waldadler (German 1m3f-1m6f inc Listed; RPR 111) and Colonel Mustard (smart bumper/2m-2m4f hurdle); dam 1m1f 2yo winner, half-sister to German Derby winner; of obvious interest for champion trainer.
runners.comment: Waldgeist gelding cost 82,000euros at three; half-brother to Colonel Mustard; big interest

runners.name: Colwyn Bay
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Optimistic Belief (1m3f) and True Companion (7f); stable 1-80 in bumpers last five seasons.
runners.comment: Dam unraced half-sister to two winners; stable 1-80 in bumpers last five seasons

runners.name: Lily Hall
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; dam last in 8.6f maiden only start; failed to complete in four of five point-to-point starts; stable had 100-1 bumper winner at Downpatrick in 2024 but he looks an unlikely winner.
runners.comment: No form in points; stable had 100-1 bumper winner in 2024 but he's an unlikely winner

runners.name: Its All Clear
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on debut at Naas in December (sent off 66-1); major improvement required.
runners.comment: Well beaten on debut at Naas in December (sent off 66-1); major improvement required
runners.owner: Paul M OLoughlin & W D Gannon

runners.name: Whatdoyoucallhim
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two bumpers in 2024; completely tailed off on return from absence at Thurles last month; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Completely tailed off on return from absence at Thurles last month; safe to rule out

races.name: Al Fateh Cup Sponsored By Bapco Energies (Handicap) (Turf)
The International Handicap (Turf)
